title: The Nicolas Cage Hall of Fame and ‘The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent’
format: Sean Fennessey, Rob Mahoney, and Van Lathan review The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent, then spend the back half of the show building a ten-film Nicolas Cage Hall of Fame out of a filmography that runs past ninety movies.

segments.heading: The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent
segments.note: Tom Gormican's meta-action comedy, Cage playing a version of himself

films.title: The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent
films.note: A broke, self-parodying Nic Cage takes $1 million to attend a superfan's (Pedro Pascal) birthday party and gets pulled into a CIA operation. Van calls it more compelling as an experiment than as a movie; Rob thinks it plays broad Hollywood satire instead of digging into Cage the way it could have. Both agree it's at its best as a hangout movie between Cage and Pascal, and both single out the box office, about $7 million on opening weekend, as a sign there's no longer a mass audience left to sell an inside-baseball Cage movie to.

segments.heading: The Nicolas Cage Hall of Fame
segments.note: chronological rundown of the filmography, sorted red / yellow / green down to a final ten

films.title: Fast Times at Ridgemont High
films.note: Cage's first film role, an uncredited fry cook billed as Brad's Bud. Immediate red.

films.title: Valley Girl
films.note: His breakout, playing Randy. Called a yellow, argued as the mandatory big-break-movie induction, ultimately cut in the final trim.

films.title: Rumble Fish
films.note: First of several films with uncle Francis Ford Coppola. A mid-tier part; not in.

films.title: Racing with the Moon
films.note: Not in.

films.title: The Cotton Club
films.note: Plays Vincent Dwyer, another Coppola picture. Called “a fascinating miss.” Not in.

films.title: Birdy
films.note: “A good movie,” but no one makes a case for it. Not in.

films.title: The Boy in Blue
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Peggy Sue Got Married
films.note: Second lead opposite Kathleen Turner, another Coppola collaboration. Yellow alongside Valley Girl, cut once Moonstruck covers the same ground better.

films.title: Raising Arizona
films.note: In the final ten. “Arguably the best movie he's been a part of.” Sean calls his H.I. McDunnough the human Wile E. Coyote.

films.title: Moonstruck
films.note: In the final ten. Plays Ronny Cammareri opposite Cher; called maybe his most charming performance and the movie that has to be in because of sheer cultural weight.

films.title: Vampire's Kiss
films.note: In the conversation for craziest Cage performance ever: he eats a live cockroach on camera. Agreed to be memorable but not enduring enough to make the cut.

films.title: Time to Kill
films.note: An Italian production, explicitly not the John Grisham adaptation of the same name. Red, and not seen by all three hosts.

films.title: Wild at Heart
films.note: In the final ten. David Lynch's Sailor Ripley. Held at yellow for most of the conversation before making the final cut over Bad Lieutenant: Port of Call New Orleans and Pig.

films.title: Fire Birds
films.note: Called a cheap Top Gun knockoff. Not in; none of the hosts have seen it.

films.title: Zandalee
films.note: A straight-to-video release. Not in; none of the hosts have seen it.

films.title: Honeymoon in Vegas
films.note: A mainstream Andrew Bergman rom-com the hosts agree “means something” to Cage's career arc. Yellow, ultimately cut.

films.title: Amos & Andrew
films.note: Cage and Samuel L. Jackson in a race satire the hosts call one of the dumbest movies they've seen. Not in.

films.title: Red Rock West
films.note: Van's pick for an underseen Cage noir; Rob makes a strong case for it as one of the actor's best, simmering performances. Held at yellow before losing out to bigger, more central films.

films.title: Deadfall
films.note: A lesser neo-noir. Not in.

films.title: Guarding Tess
films.note: Not in.

films.title: It Could Happen to You
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Trapped in Paradise
films.note: Van has real affection for it as a childhood favorite, but it's not in.

films.title: Kiss of Death
films.note: A “batshit” remake of the 1947 noir with Samuel L. Jackson and Michael Rapaport. Loved as a viewing experience, not in the Hall.

films.title: Leaving Las Vegas
films.note: In the final ten. Cage's Best Actor Oscar win; automatic, no debate.

films.title: The Rock
films.note: In the final ten. First-ballot, no questions asked.

films.title: Con Air
films.note: In the final ten. The subject of the episode's biggest argument: Rob initially ranks it third behind The Rock and Face/Off, Van fights hard for it, and it survives into the final ten.

films.title: Face/Off
films.note: In the final ten. John Woo's Castor Troy. Automatically in, no debate.

films.title: City of Angels
films.note: A $198 million hit opposite Meg Ryan. Argued hard for by Van given its box office, but cut from the final ten.

films.title: Snake Eyes
films.note: Brian De Palma thriller Sean calls one of the most incredible pieces of filmmaking of the late '90s, but he declines to fight for its inclusion. Not in.

films.title: 8MM
films.note: Van's a defender of this one, but it's not in.

films.title: Bringing Out the Dead
films.note: Cage's one Martin Scorsese collaboration. Called a really good performance in a movie that came out in the shadow of Casino. Yellow, ultimately cut.

films.title: Gone in Sixty Seconds
films.note: A stylish, empty hit later overshadowed by the Fast and the Furious franchise. Yellow, ultimately cut.

films.title: The Family Man
films.note: Rob's introduction to Cage as an 11-year-old. Not in the Hall.

films.title: Captain Corelli's Mandolin
films.note: Referenced for the Unbearable Weight joke made at its expense. Not in.

films.title: Windtalkers
films.note: A reunion with John Woo that wasn't a success. Not in.

films.title: Sonny
films.note: Cage's directorial debut; he also has a small role as Acid Yellow. Not in.

films.title: Adaptation
films.note: In the final ten. Spike Jonze's Charlie and Donald Kaufman, an Oscar-nominated dual performance the group calls his most transformational. Automatic in.

films.title: Matchstick Men
films.note: In the final ten. Ridley Scott's con-man drama. The last spot in the final ten comes down to this versus National Treasure and Pig; Sean, as decider, picks Matchstick Men.

films.title: National Treasure
films.note: A $350 million Jerry Bruckheimer blockbuster. Debated hard as the populist pick for the final spot: Van argues for it on scale, Rob and Sean ultimately go with Matchstick Men instead.

films.title: Lord of War
films.note: Well liked by the hosts but agreed not to be a Hall of Fame movie.

films.title: The Weather Man
films.note: Van's personal favorite of the whole filmography, but the group agrees it's not making the final cut.

films.title: The Ant Bully
films.note: A voice role. Not in.

films.title: World Trade Center
films.note: Oliver Stone's 9/11 drama, called sincere and well made but not great. Not in.

films.title: The Wicker Man
films.note: Neil LaBute's remake, source of the most-memed Cage clip of all (the bees). Not a Hall of Fame movie on its own merits, though the group jokes the meme itself deserves a spot in the lobby.

films.title: Ghost Rider
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Grindhouse
films.note: Cage cameos as Fu Manchu in the Werewolf Women of the SS fake trailer. Not in.

films.title: Next
films.note: Lee Tomahori thriller Sean watched for the first time this week and found surprisingly bad. Not in.

films.title: National Treasure: Book of Secrets
films.note: Cage's highest-grossing U.S. film. Not in; the hosts explicitly reject grosses as a qualifying metric.

films.title: Bangkok Dangerous
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Knowing
films.note: Van was surprised by how much it worked for him at the time. Not in.

films.title: G-Force
films.note: A voice role, a gerbil. Not in.

films.title: Astro Boy
films.note: A voice role, Dr. Tenma. Not in.

films.title: Bad Lieutenant: Port of Call New Orleans
films.note: Werner Herzog's loose sequel to the Abel Ferrara film. Called one of Cage's best performances of the period; held at yellow, ultimately cut.

films.title: Kick-Ass
films.note: Cage plays Big Daddy. Not in.

films.title: The Sorcerer's Apprentice
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Season of the Witch
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Drive Angry
films.note: Sean says he kind of likes it. Not in.

films.title: Seeking Justice
films.note: None of the hosts have seen it. Not in.

films.title: Trespass
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Ghost Rider: Spirit of Vengeance
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Stolen
films.note: Not in.

films.title: The Croods
films.note: A voice role (Grug) and a hit. Not in.

films.title: The Frozen Ground
films.note: Cage plays a detective hunting a serial killer in Alaska. Not in.

films.title: Joe
films.note: David Gordon Green drama, referenced again via a cameo from Green in Unbearable Weight. Not in.

films.title: Rage
films.note: None of the hosts have seen it. Not in.

films.title: Outcast
films.note: None of the hosts have seen it. Not in.

films.title: Left Behind
films.note: The Christian-apocalypse adaptation, to the hosts' shock. Not in.

films.title: Dying of the Light
films.note: A Paul Schrader film reportedly re-edited against his wishes. Not in.

films.title: The Runner
films.note: None of the hosts have seen it. Not in.

films.title: Pay the Ghost
films.note: None of the hosts have seen it. Not in.

films.title: Snowden
films.note: A rare supporting role in a big-director mainstream film that none of the hosts can recall much about. Not in.

films.title: Dog Eat Dog
films.note: A second Schrader collaboration, with Willem Dafoe. Called a fun, grimy crime movie, not a Hall of Fame one.

films.title: USS Indianapolis: Men of Courage
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Army of One
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Arsenal
films.note: None of the hosts have seen it. Not in.

films.title: Vengeance: A Love Story
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Inconceivable
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Mom and Dad
films.note: A horror-comedy with Selma Blair. Called pretty fun. Not in.

films.title: Mandy
films.note: In the final ten. Panos Cosmatos's psychedelic revenge film, credited as the start of the modern Cage-aissance. Held at “yellow minimum” and argued as too central to Cage's story to leave out; makes the final cut.

films.title: Looking Glass
films.note: Not in.

films.title: The Humanity Bureau
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Teen Titans GO! To the Movies
films.note: A voice cameo as Superman, an in-joke referencing Cage's unmade Tim Burton Superman film. Not in.

films.title: Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse
films.note: A voice role as Spider-Man Noir. Not in the Hall, but singled out as a great reveal.

films.title: Between Worlds
films.note: Cage plays Joe Majors. Not in.

films.title: A Score to Settle
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Color Out of Space
films.note: An H.P. Lovecraft adaptation the group singles out earlier as a rare bad Cage performance in an otherwise interesting movie. Not in.

films.title: Running with the Devil
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Kill Chain
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Primal
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Grand Isle
films.note: Not in.

films.title: Jiu Jitsu
films.note: A pandemic watch Sean recalls fondly as a lark. Not in.

films.title: The Croods: A New Age
films.note: A voice-role sequel. Not in.

films.title: Prisoners of the Ghostland
films.note: The Sion Sono film that premiered at Sundance. Not in.

films.title: Willy's Wonderland
films.note: A Chuck E. Cheese-style riff. Not in.

films.title: Pig
films.note: Rob's clear favorite of the two 2021-2022 “Cage return” films and the film he fights hardest for; loses the final spot to Matchstick Men and Mandy.

films.title: The Unbearable Weight of Massive Talent
films.note: Reviewed earlier in the episode. Explicitly kept out of the Hall of Fame.

films: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb
